page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg7.24xlarge is a high-frequency general-purpose instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs, utilizing Intel Xeon 8369HB processors running at a base clock speed of 3.3 GHz. With a 1:1 ratio of threads to physical cores, this instance delivers dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading overhead.

The instance is configured with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 32 Gbps, facilitating efficient data transfer rates for distributed environments.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated core allocation and high clock speed offer predictable compute performance, making it highly cost-efficient for workloads that require consistent CPU execution without virtualization jitter. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, optimizing the instance for pure CPU-bound processing and memory-balanced workloads. Suitable use cases include high-performance web serving, enterprise application hosting, and distributed compute tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg7.24xlarge is a high-frequency general-purpose instance designed for workloads requiring dedicated compute resources. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 96 dedicated physical Intel Xeon 8369HB CPU cores running at 3.3 GHz with a single thread per core configuration. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It contains no local storage or GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage options. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 32 Gbps. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy applications that benefit from high clock speeds and dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading. It is well-suited for enterprise applications, web serving, and distributed processing workloads.
